description: `ref`, when present, is a non-empty space-separated command path resolving in the artifact's command tree; each segment matches a command's declared name or any of its declared aliases, exactly and case-sensitively. The root command is addressed by omitting `ref`; an empty-string `ref` is not conformant.
notes: Resolution-dependent tests always embed the descriptor as content, so the verdict is offline-decidable; verifying them requires a usage (KDL) descriptor parser, and a validator without one reports those tests unverified rather than passing or failing them (core partial-verification posture). Grammar tests (empty ref, empty segment) are decidable without the artifact.

tests.description: single-segment command path resolving to a declared command
multi-segment command path walking the tree from the root command
segment matching a command's declared alias (m for migrate), exactly
omitted ref addresses the root command
empty-string ref is not conformant: this specification gives each meaning one spelling (the root command is addressed by omitting ref)
double space in the command path produces an empty segment: segments are separated by single spaces
command path resolving to no command in the embedded descriptor makes the binding unresolvable
segment matching is case-sensitive: Status does not match the declared command status
